title: Neolemonniera
intro: Neolemonniera is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Neolemonniera include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been found to exhibit potent anticancer, antiviral, and antimalarial activities, as well as other compounds such as alkaloids and flavonoids. Neolemonniera is typically processed into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall health.

Its medicinal properties are often attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. In some parts of the world, Neolemonniera is used as a remedy for fever and sore throats. Additionally, it is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers.
